entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 20 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-20 03:44AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • It was a 19th century classic, but Lost Illusions says much about today
  • Lightscape festival to mesmerise at Botanic Gardens
  • Australia news LIVE: Former foreign minister says Australia should demand Assange release; Omicron wave was twice the size of recorded cases
  • Police investigate alleged sexual assault at WA mine site
  • 'Razor' Robertson's latest breakdancing celebration
  • Google co-founder drops $18m on Pink’s former Malibu estate
  • A confrontation at a fish and chip shop leaves a man fighting for life
  • May, Melksham available for selection, Powell injury scares Suns, Preuss suspended
  • Clive Palmer’s candidate claims last Victorian Senate spot
  • Saudi seizes rainbow items in homosexuality crackdown
  • Skelton red card makes unwanted history
  • Major police operation will continue in Sydney's north-west today after undercover officers attacked
  • The rise and rise of Tony Armstrong, TV’s most popular new star
  • Swimming bans transgender athletes from women's events
  • States to decide whether gas and coal are included in a national energy scheme
  • Macron projected to lose parliamentary majority
  • Great unknown: Markets brace for turmoil as the Fed unwinds its 14-year experiment
  • The property type defying the downturn, for now
  • NRL key takeouts: Round 15
  • Barbarians captain nails cheeky backheel conversion
  • Best of cartoons, June 20, 2022
  • ‘Slow and careful’: Four weeks in and no major hitches for Albanese
  • Australia should demand US release of Assange, says Carr
  • Health minister flags changes to international vaccine passports
  • Coal, gas to smooth transition to clean power: Top energy officials
  • Best of cartoons, June 19, 2022
  • Hospital kits add to Ukraine aid as Australian donations pass $5m
  • Whale Beach residents rally against proposed restaurant
  • Seven charged after police raid Blockade Australia’s Blue Mountains gathering
  • Man charged after allegedly setting another on fire near Kiama
  • ‘A balancing act’: Bronte surf club calms storm over revamp
  • ‘Perilous state’: Dozens of areas across NSW at risk of critical GP shortages
  • $100m to be stripped from upgrade of Sydney’s Central Station
  • ICAC withdraws brief of evidence against Eman Sharobeem
  • PM’s intervention crucial to save Assange from life in jail
  • From the Archives, 1952: Bringing the waters under control
  • NSW urged to consider private cash for big infrastructure
  • Check the expiry date on your blood
  • Dawson’s lawyers object to new evidence about hospital where Lynette was ‘seen’
  • New ferry wharves, ‘New York-style’ high line slated for Circular Quay
  • National Trust accuses Education Department of lax attitude to heritage
  • Two arrested in police raid on climate activist group Blockade Australia
  • January 6 inquiry shines a light on the perilous state of US democracy
  • Germany to reboot mothballed coal-power plants after Putin throttles gas supply
  • Former guerilla becomes Colombia’s first leftist president
  • ‘Dangerous beach season’ kicks off on Ukrainian coast laced with explosive mines
  • Caught the first Omicron wave? Study says you can still catch the latest variant
  • ‘Crash’ director Paul Haggis detained in Italy over sexual assault charges
  • Macron faces hung parliament as support surges for far-left, far-right
  • Could Trump be charged over the US Capitol attack?
  • Google urged to stop directing people to fake abortion clinics
  • Jokes and theatre aside, the prosecution of Donald Trump has already begun
  • QPS accused of ‘discrimination’ against recruit with ADHD
  • Venom gel: How deadly snakes could soon save lives and heal wounds
  • The Age photos of the week, June 17, 2022
  • Police investigating after woman found dead in Brisbane home
  • In the market for a puppy in Perth? Read this first
  • WA taxpayers will pay $85 million to save the government $12 million
  • The Royal Commission into Defence and Veteran Suicide will begin in Townsville today
  • How snake venom could save lives on the battlefield and the hospital bed
  • First Omicron wave was really twice the size of recorded cases, study says
  • PointsBet soars on $94m injection by financial trading giant SIG
  • Aussie crypto casino strikes record sponsorship deal with Everton FC
  • ASX falls after flat start with energy and mining stocks declining
  • How former South Sydney player Wes Maas built a $600 million fortune
  • Journalists question News Corp’s prediction tool
  • Mince on the menu as grocery costs, mortgage payments bite
  • Economic times are tricky, but they’re far from ‘dire’
  • Southern Cross TV deal in limbo, buyers wait
  • Qantas, Airbus make sustainable fuel pledge
  • Directors quit at billionaire Judith Neilson’s journalism institute
  • Family, comfy shoes and cinnamon buns: What Sarah Abo can’t live without
  • The notion of the ‘good divorce’ is at the heart of Abi Morgan’s drama
  • Spoilt brats get a reality check in TV’s newest gotcha series
  • ‘This house needs a family again’: Wendy Whiteley’s extraordinary $100 million gift to NSW
  • Wendy Whiteley: ‘I want to be remembered with affection’
  • Logies 2022 as it happened: Hamish Blake wins Gold; Tony Armstrong nabs first Logie
  • Hamish Blake wins the 2022 Gold Logie
  • Hamish Blake takes out Gold Logie as TV’s biggest night returns after two years
  • Jessica Mauboy performs at 2022 Logies
  • Devastating portrait of boyhood friendship is surprise winner at Sydney Film Festival
  • The last hurrah of Colin Lanceley, peer of Arthur Boyd and Sidney Nolan
  • Produced remarkable, distinctive prints and drawings
  • Yes, Adam Sandler is a great actor (and it’s OK to admit it)
  • ‘Australians should not be misled’: Health Minister lashes cosmetic cowboys
  • Data next battle for Facebook, other social media giants
  • National weather forecast for Monday June 20
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russian Forces Tighten Noose Around Important Cities in Ukraine’s East
  • Gustavo Petro Wins the Election, Becoming Colombia’s First Leftist Leader

  • Scenes from the polls as Rodolfo Hernández and Gustavo Petro cast their ballots.

  • As Colombia votes, at stake is the fate of the third largest nation in Latin America.

  • With Child Care Scarce, States Try to Fix ‘a Broken Market’
  • Heat Wave to Shift East After Posing Dangerous Conditions in the Plains

  • The Week in Business: The Fed Goes Big
  • ‘Lightyear’ Opens at No. 2, as ‘Jurassic World’ Holds Strong
  • Tattoo Artists Face a Grayer Palette in Europe
  • A French nuclear crisis frustrates Europe’s push to quit Russian energy.
  • Tracking the Fear Index on Wall Street
  • How Can You Travel for $100 or Less a Day? Get a Cruise Deal.
News from the New York Times

entry.content:

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 20 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-20 03:06AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Rusty’ aviation industry needs time to get flowing again, with passengers facing higher ticket prices amid rising fuel costs and staff shortages, Qantas chief warns
  • Inspiring memories of Harrow International’s first decade
  • Beijing underlines its jurisdiction and desire for urgent action by laying down targets for incoming Hong Kong leader, analysts say
  • HKEX to offer more A-share derivatives and financial products to affirm Hong Kong’s role as China’s offshore capital hub, Aguzin says
  • Coronavirus: elderly Hongkongers turn to expressive arts therapy to ease pandemic blues
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee vows US sanctions will not interfere with safeguarding national security
  • Who will help John Lee run Hong Kong? A quick guide to old and new faces on his governing team
  • Resuming quarantine-free travel with mainland China to be top priority, Hong Kong’s incoming health minister says
  • URA to spend HK$500 million renovating 4 older areas of Hong Kong, with focus on preventive maintenance of ageing buildings
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong’s border control rules untenable, Carrie Lam says, while city logs 1,276 cases
  • Earthquake rattles Taiwan, no immediate damage reported
  • China resists loan rate cuts, keeps lending benchmark unchanged in June
  • ‘Mind-reading’ device to detect porn could speed China’s policing of illicit content, say researchers

  • How the US’ Xinjiang labour law has crippled China’s cotton industry before even entering effect
  • China chalks up success in push to promote favoured candidates on international bodies
  • China names Han ex-environmental protection officer to head ethnic affairs office
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping says country has won an ‘overwhelming’ victory in corruption battle – but warns more work is needed to tackle problem
  • Disappeared Chinese coal tycoon who took on top judge to hear appeal verdict
  • China scientists find a grain of hope in climate change rice research
  • China urges IMF to approve US$1.4 billion Zambia bailout, but told it must reach deal with other lenders
  • Burger King Japan swaps French fries for ramen after running low on potatoes
  • Will Malaysia see a fresh influx of Rohingya refugees as life in Bangladesh, Myanmar gets worse?
  • Duterte’s daughter Sara sworn in early as vice-president of Philippines in show of independence
  • Japan: 5.2 magnitude earthquake hits west coast’s Ishikawa, ‘no tsunami threat’
  • Japan debates getting a nuclear submarine – but Kishida’s ‘not so sure’
  • From rail yoga to doga and snowga, 5,000-year-old Indian practice takes many bends and twists
  • From Australia to India, massive passenger demand sparks travel chaos
  • Australia to get US-made nuclear submarines by 2030, opposition leader claims
  • Thailand’s looking to legalise same-sex unions, but will it embrace full marriage equality?
  • Colombia elects former guerilla Gustavo Petro as first leftist president
  • Bitcoin climbs 7.6 per cent to pass US$20,400, signalling recovery from recent drop
  • EU seeks to release Ukrainian grain stuck due to Russia’s sea blockade
  • Ukraine war: ‘Fair trial’ urged for Moroccan fighter facing death penalty
  • World swimming’s governing body bans transgender athletes from women’s events
  • France’s Emmanuel Macron loses parliament majority in stunning setback
  • Oscar-winning director Paul Haggis detained in Italy over sexual assault claims
  • After British journalist’s murder in the Amazon, can his vision survive?
  • Israel’s foreign minister Yair Lapid to visit Türkiye amid security jitters
  • US: China ‘engages in unfair trade practices’ but tariffs serve ‘no strategic purpose’
  • ‘True Independence Day’: US parades, speeches, commemorate ‘end of slavery’
  • Kelly Holmes, 52, British Olympic athletics champion, comes out as gay after ‘years of heartache’
  • Germany plans for more coal amid Russia gas supply drop: ‘bitter but necessary’
  • ‘Lessons to be learned for Ukraine’: Dutch PM apologises to Bosnia peacekeepers
  • Apple workers vote to form tech giant’s first-ever US union
  • France faces chaos without me, Macron tells voters
  • Ukraine war ‘could take years’, Nato chief warns
  • NetEase delays highly anticipated release of Diablo Immortal in big setback for China’s No. 2 gaming company amid ongoing Weibo ban
  • Hong Kong stocks slip as Macau casino operators tumble amid new outbreak while China refrains from cutting rates
  • How Japan’s foray into a digital yen could be its key to the Web 3.0 universe
  • Singapore office-design firm Space Matrix acquires B2B e-commerce site Pursuite amid strong demand for flexible workspaces
  • China-focused funds reload Alibaba, JD.com stocks to defy ‘uninvestable’ tag as Goldman’s worst-is-over view gains traction
  • Nearly all of Wall Street – and the Fed – botched calls for 2022 as stocks, Treasuries, bitcoin slump in tandem
  • Climate change: China’s state firms take lead in advocating carbon neutrality, must do more about own emissions, experts say
  • Climate-change: BlackRock votes against fewer directors as more companies adopt TCFD framework for risk disclosures, panel hears
  • How artificial intelligence will redefine the future of work
  • Australia’s Aboriginal history celebrated in stunning new Sydney monument overlooking the opera house
  • Aphasia: what it is, how it happens and how to help those suffering from it and related conditions
  • Cities through a pilot’s eyes – folksy and scholarly musings on urban landscapes seen fleetingly
  • Hailey Bieber launches a celebrity skincare brand, Rhode, and Kim Kardashian’s Skkn by Kim is coming right up. They join a crowded field
  • How to age well: mature dad shed 15kg in 6 months to keep up with his toddler son; follow this newly fit doctor’s tips to lose weight
  • Thailand, land of smiles ... and chocolate. Home-grown cacao beans and locally made bars embraced in Bangkok
  • Meeting Muhammad Ali, tussling with a tennis legend, living easy on Lamma – Nick Lovatt the bearded book man on his 70 years in Hong Kong
  • Radhika Merchant’s big dance debut! Mukesh and Nita Ambani’s future daughter-in-law dazzled celebrities from Salman Khan to Ranveer Singh in her arangetram, hosted by Anant and family
  • 18 celebrities who came out as LGBT in 2022, so far: from Rebel Wilson and Selling Sunset’s Chrishell Stause to music stars Janelle Monáe and Emeli Sandé, and RuPaul’s Drag Race queens Bosco and Willow Pill
  • Who is bitcoin billionaire Michael Saylor? The MicroStrategy CEO’s net worth once dropped by US$6 billion in one day during the dot-com boom … will the same happen with his crypto investments?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.
